title: rosa canina subsp. pouzinii
intro: Rosa canina subsp. pouzinii, also known as the dwarf dog rose, is a subspecies of the dog rose plant, characterized by its compact growth habit and small, white to pink flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can be attributed to its high content of vitamin C and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rosa canina subsp. pouzinii include its ability to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and improve cardiovascular health, making it a valuable herb in the treatment of various conditions such as colds, flu, and eczema. Rosa canina subsp. pouzinii can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed as a supplement or used topically in creams and ointments.
